budget_status: EXHAUSTED as pre-declared: 10 of 10 runs used (6 of 8 variants), accounted dev spend USD 4.4186 of the 8.00 cap; closure per amendment 7 and the consult #25 precommitment (no label-responsive tuning after v6)
headline: prompt development took gemma-4-31B from useless (reject recall 0.63, 54 false-allows under the frozen one-line prompt) to the gate boundary (v4: allow 0.937-0.957 by denominator, reject 0.979-1.000, false-allows 0-3 per run); no configuration met every frozen gate stably

stability_finding.method: three full v4 replicates at temperature 0, seed 0, identical bytes
stability_finding.result: 10 of 350 items flip verdicts across replicates (7 labeled allow, 3 labeled reject); per-replicate false-allows 0 / 1 / 3; majority-of-three still leaves 1 false-allow (1792ed27efb65aa2, allowed in 2 of 3 runs)
stability_finding.interpretation: the provider endpoint is not deterministic at temperature 0; observed zero-false-allows in any single run is a sample property, not a stable configuration property; the best configuration's residual per-run false-allow rate is roughly 1 in 145 (point ~0.7%, small-sample upper bound a few percent), concentrated in 2-3 boundary structures (jurisdiction-scope joins, causal joins) plus stochastic flips

frozen_configuration_proposal.status: PROPOSED - awaiting owner ratification at the close-out check-in
frozen_configuration_proposal.prompt_sha256_of_variant_string: computed at ratification from rejudge/phase2_checker_dev_runner.py VARIANTS['v4_merged']
frozen_configuration_proposal.parser: strict exact lower-case single token; empty completion gets one paid re-ask (transport artifact rule)
frozen_configuration_proposal.transport: v5 pins (streaming for reasoning models, sdk_internal_max_retries=0, http timeouts, halt_on_unknown_charge)
frozen_configuration_proposal.selection_basis: only configuration with zero false-allows on both scoring denominators in its first run and reject recall 0.979-1.000 across all three replicates; v5/v6 traded single false-allows for allow recall

owner_decision_menu.a_freeze_and_holdout: ratify the v4 freeze and run the consult #25 holdout (fresh cluster-separated records, ~412 candidates for >=149 rejects at 95% confidence, labels sealed before outputs, one run, retry-inclusive protocol scored; estimated provider cost under USD 1.50, labeling is assistant labor); the holdout verdict then reports observed rates with replicate-aware language rather than claiming deterministic zero
owner_decision_menu.b_human_gate: drop the LLM checker for phase 2: mechanical screen plus 100% human approval of every allowed or unresolved query (consult #24's fallback); zero model risk, real Jack/Joey workload per run
owner_decision_menu.c_gate_amendment: amend the zero-false-allow gate to an explicit rate bound with mandatory human audit of allows (requires a new oracle consult; #24 warned against relaxation as a rescue and required the operational control to change if the threshold does)
owner_decision_menu.recommendation: a, with the runtime protections already in the frozen protocol (mechanical screen first, one free retry, unresolved halts, human audit sampling) stated as the layered defense; b remains the fallback if the holdout fails

note: Append-only. The dev ledger, outputs, and variant log are the complete audit trail; no labels were changed at any point during development.
